reviews.comment:
La recontra mega mejor onda, tanto el del surtidor como en la cafetería. Para los viajeros, tiene Habitaciones que no use, enchufes para cargar celular, y una taza de café que es un tazón de sopa! Lugar obligado si vas de Argentina hacia Chile ya que en chile no me recibieron dólares, solo te reciben pesos chilenos y es súper importante tenés habilitada la tarjeta para el viaje ya que nadie te cambia dólares. Tuve que cambiar en un almacén, a un cambio malísimo, pero no tuve elección.

Porque? Facil estaba de paso a Perú y la tarjeta no me funcionó en chile. Mi consejo al pasar a chile por lo menos deben tener el equivalente de un tanque en pesos chilenos, o que les funcione la tarjeta en ese país, porque nadie te cambia. Y la autonomía no da para llegar a Perú.

PD: hablando de autonomía, NO MIREN GOOGLE. El mapa les tira una cantidad de kilómetros, pero no son kilómetros de ruta, porque hay muchísimas partes que deben bajar a cero prácticamente porque en mi caso debía andar en 2nda o hasta 1era en muchas curvas que son curva cerrada ciega y en subida en helicoide, por lo tanto no se puede pasar a velocidad. Y cuando les toque bajada, más vale que usen el freno motor si no quieren pasar al otro mundo volando como un ave. Las normas de seguridad hagan de cuenta que aquí no existen.

(Translated by Google)
The vibe was absolutely fantastic, both at the gas station and in the café. For travelers, they have rooms I didn't use, outlets for charging phones, and a coffee cup that's more like a bowl of soup! A must-stop if you're traveling from Argentina to Chile, since they wouldn't accept dollars in Chile, only Chilean pesos, and it's super important to have your card activated for travel because nobody will exchange dollars. I had to exchange money at a corner store, at a terrible rate, but I had no choice.

Why? Simple: I was passing through to Peru and my card didn't work in Chile. My advice when crossing into Chile is to at least have the equivalent of a tank of gas in Chilean pesos, or make sure your card works there, because nobody will exchange money. And the range isn't enough to get you to Peru.

PS: Speaking of range, DON'T GOOGLE IT. The map shows you a certain number of kilometers, but those aren't actual kilometers of road, because there are many sections where you practically have to slow down to zero. In my case, I had to drive in second or even first gear on many blind, hairpin turns and uphill hairpin bends, so you can't take them at speed. And when you come to a downhill section, you'd better use engine braking if you don't want to be blown to bits like a bird. Safety rules don't exist here.
reviews.name: accounts/109853746269412609224/locations/1243722777492158217/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUM3aWZDX2J3EAE

Não tem qualidade de um YPF, frentista mal educado e desatento com suas obrigações, com serviço de má qualidade.
Agradeço pelo wi-fi que ajuda muito quem precisa de internet em uma cidade sem sinal de telefone.

reviews.comment:
(Translated by Google) Terrible experience. The gas station attendants refused to fill up when paying by card, saying the system was down. After we said we were going to pay in dollars, they laughed and said we could use a card. This happened both ways and with other Brazilians who were filling up. A completely disrespectful place, but unfortunately we were stuck there because it was the only option for refueling, with no other gas stations available. Brazilians, be careful not to be scammed at this place.

(Original)
Péssima experiência, os frentistas se recusam a abastecer quando o pagamento é em cartão, informam que está sem sistema. Depois que falamos que íamos pagar com dólar, eles riram e falaram que poderia passar cartão. Isso aconteceu na ida e volta e com mais brasileiros que estavam abastecendo. Local totalmente desrespeitoso, mas infelizmente ficamos reféns, pois é a única opção para abastecer, sem opção de outros postos de combustíveis. Brasileiros, fiquem atentos para não serem enganados nesse lugar.
